source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/05_해외 등록 서류 (영업팀 F-up)/240515 ASPEN IODR FDA 대응/Abyz-FDA-20240515/#Abyz/12. Software Valiadtion Report/Stitching 평가보고서/006_Smart Stitching Auto Stitching Performance Report_failure explained.docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Performance report for Smart Stitching auto stitching using images acquired by X-ray systems.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports software validation by documenting test conditions, methods, acceptance criteria, stitching performance results, and failure explanation.
keyPoints: Test items assess stitching performance for images acquired by X-ray system.
The number of stitching images should be 2~5.
Acceptance criteria require auto stitching error to be less than 15 pixels each in horizontal and vertical direction compared with manual stitching.
Manual stitching images are obtained from radiologist Kim Joo-Young, and radiologist confirmation is required.
The reported cause of image stitching failure is patient movement during X-ray image acquisition.
The report states auto stitching performance is affected by stitching parameters and image characteristics.

entities.name: Kim Joo-Young
entities.evidence: Obtain manual stitching images from radiologist(Kim Joo-Young).

entities.name: Kodak CR975
entities.evidence: X-ray Mfr Kodak Type (CR/DR) CR Model CR975

concepts.name: X-ray system image stitching
concepts.evidence: Assessment of stitching performance for image acquired by X-ray system

concepts.name: Manual stitching comparison
concepts.evidence: Compared with manual stitching, the error range for auto stitching should be less than 15 pixels each in the horizontal and vertical direction.

concepts.name: Stitching failure due to patient movement
concepts.pageTarget: Patient Movement During X-ray Image Acquisition
concepts.evidence: The cause of the failure in image stitching has been analyzed to be patient movement during the acquisition of x-ray images.

concepts.name: Auto stitching parameters
concepts.evidence: Therefore, the setting of the auto stitching parameter according to the image characteristics is important for stitching performance.

concepts.name: DICOM image import test method
concepts.evidence: Download the DICOM image to a local storage. Import the locally stored image and perform the test.

evidence.claim: The document is an Auto Stitching Performance Report for Smart Stitching.
evidence.evidenceText: Auto Stitching Performance Report; S / W Title Smart Stitching

evidence.claim: The report author is Hyun Ji Cho.
The test title is Smart Stitching performance per X-ray System.
evidence.evidenceText: Test Title - Smart Stitching performance per X-ray System
evidence.pageTarget: Smart Stitching Performance Per X-ray System

evidence.claim: The test assesses stitching performance for images acquired by X-ray systems.
evidence.evidenceText: Test Items - Assessment of stitching performance for image acquired by X-ray system

evidence.claim: The test condition used Microsoft Windows 10 Pro 64-bit.
The test condition used Intel Core i5-6500 CPU, 8GB RAM, NVIDIA Geforce GT 730 graphics card, and 1TB HDD.
evidence.evidenceText: CPU : Intel(R) Core(™) i5-6500 CPU @3.20GHz 3.19GHz; RAM : 8GB; Graphic Card : NVIDIA Geforce GT 730; HDD : 1TB

evidence.claim: The test method downloads DICOM images to local storage, imports them, and performs the test.
evidence.evidenceText: Download the DICOM image to a local storage. Import the locally stored image and perform the test.

evidence.claim: The number of stitching images should be 2 to 5.
evidence.evidenceText: The number of stitching images should be 2~5.

evidence.claim: Auto stitching acceptance criteria require less than 15 pixels error in horizontal and vertical direction compared with manual stitching.
evidence.evidenceText: Compared with manual stitching, the error range for auto stitching should be less than 15 pixels each in the horizontal and vertical direction.

evidence.claim: Manual stitching images are obtained from radiologist Kim Joo-Young.
evidence.evidenceText: Obtain manual stitching images from radiologist(Kim Joo-Young).

evidence.claim: For spine image capture, vertebrae counts must be correct for C-Spine 7, T-Spine 12, and L-Spine 5.
evidence.evidenceText: For Spine image capture, the number of vertebrae (C-Spine: 7, T-Spine: 12, L-Spine: 5) should be correct.

evidence.claim: Radiologist confirmation is required for vertebrae count and tape measure alignment criteria.
evidence.evidenceText: Get confirmation from the radiologist; If there is a tape measure, it should be evenly aligned. [See the Figure 3] Get confirmation from the radiologist.

evidence.claim: If any one of the listed acceptance conditions is not met, the result is regarded as failure.
evidence.evidenceText: If any one of 1~3 conditions is not met, it is regarded as failure.

evidence.claim: Based on two input images, auto stitching time is about one second.
evidence.evidenceText: Based on 2 input images, the auto stitching time is about 1 second.

evidence.claim: The analyzed cause of image stitching failure is patient movement during X-ray image acquisition.
evidence.evidenceText: The cause of the failure in image stitching has been analyzed to be patient movement during the acquisition of x-ray images. See [Table 1] No.1, No. 3
evidence.pageTarget: Patient Movement During X-ray Image Acquisition

evidence.claim: Auto stitching results are greatly affected by the auto stitching parameter, and optimized parameter values may differ by image redundant area location.
evidence.evidenceText: The auto stitching result is greatly affected by the auto stitching parameter and the optimized parameter value may be different depending on the location of the redundant area of the image.
Input parameters affect the stitching speed. -See [Table1] No.1~No.16

evidence.claim: The setting of auto stitching parameters according to image characteristics is important for stitching performance.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, the setting of the auto stitching parameter according to the image characteristics is important for stitching performance.
